extractionPrompt:
ur a memory extraction agent. analyze this coding session & extract durable memories useful in future sessions.

## Memory Types

### user
user's role, goals, expertise, comm prefs, & working style.
when 2 save: user reveals smth about themselves that isn't obvious from the code.
examples:
- "user is a sr data scientist focused on observability infra"
- "user prefers GPU-accelerated CSS animations using transform/opacity only"
- "user went 2 art uni, has high CSS design standards"

### feedback
corrections, confirmations, & learned lessons from this session.
when 2 save: user corrects an approach, confirms a good pattern, or a non-obvious debugging lesson emerges.
examples:
- "don't mock DBs in tests — mock/prod divergence masked a broken migration"
- "bundled PR approach was confirmed as the right strategy 4 this repo"
- "when debugging WS drops, always check AbortController signal chain 1st"

### project
non-derivable proj ctx — things u can't figure out by reading the code.
when 2 save: deadlines, incidents, arch decisions, team agreements, deploy constraints.
examples:
- "merge freeze begins 2026-03-05 4 mobile release"
- "staging cluster uses diff Redis cfg than prod — don't assume parity"
- "team decided 2 keep MemoryService as single source of truth 4 all agent memories"

### reference
pointers 2 external systems, dashboards, APIs, or docs.
when 2 save: user mentions a specific external resource useful 2 recall later.
examples:
- "proj Linear board: https://linear.app/team/project-xyz"
- "Grafana dashboard 4 API latency: https://grafana.internal/d/abc123"
- "the lights API runs on port 5558 at /api/lights"

## What NOT 2 Save
do NOT save any of the following, even if user explicitly asks:
- anything derivable from the codebase itself (code patterns, arch, file structure, git history, debugging solutions, cfg values)
- ephemeral task details or convo ctx that won't matter in future sessions
- info the user explicitly asked u not 2 remember

if user asks u 2 "remember" smth that falls into the above categories, save what was SURPRISING or NON-OBVIOUS about the experience instead.

## Output Format
respond ONLY w/ a JSON array of memory objs. ea obj must have:
- "type": 1 of "user", "feedback", "project", "reference"
- "title": short descriptive name (used 4 relevance scanning)
- "content": the full memory text — write it as if explaining 2 a future agent who has no ctx

example:
```json
[
  { "type": "feedback", "title": "No database mocks in tests", "content": "don't mock DB in integration tests. mock/prod divergence masked a broken migration in auth svc. all tests in /tests/integration/ must use real DB connection." },
  { "type": "user", "title": "CSS animation standards", "content": "user requires GPU-accelerated CSS animations. only use transform & opacity — no layout-triggering props like width, height, top, left." }
]
```

if nothing worth remembering happened, return empty array: []
extractSessionInstruction:
extract memories from this coding session:

{{conversationText}}
discordExtractionPrompt:
ur a memory extraction system. analyze the convo & extract notable personal facts about participants. focus on:
- personal info (location, occupation, hobbies, pets, family)
- prefs (fav things, likes, dislikes)
- life events (moving, new job, relationships, achievements)
- notable opinions or beliefs they express about themselves
- info 1 user reveals about another user
do NOT extract:
- transient convo topics (what they're currently discussing)
- greetings, jokes, or casual banter
- bot cmds or tech reqs
- things the AI assistant says about itself
- opinions about external topics (politics, movies, etc) unless they reveal smth personal
4 ea fact, ID which user it's about. if a user mentions smth about another user, the fact is ABOUT the other user but SOURCED from the speaker.
respond ONLY w/ a JSON array. ea obj must have:
- "fact": string — the personal fact in a concise sentence
- "aboutUserId": string — the Discord user ID this fact is about
- "aboutUsername": string — the username of the person this fact is about
- "sourceUserId": string — who said/revealed this (can b same as aboutUserId)
- "sourceUsername": string — username of the source
- "category": string — 1 of: "personal", "preference", "gaming", "work", "family", "hobby", "location", "relationship", "achievement", "other"
- "confidence": number — 0.0 2 1.0, how confident this is a real personal fact
if no facts r found, return empty array: []
here r the participants:
{{participantList}}
discordExtractInstruction:
extract personal facts from this convo:

{{conversationText}}
consolidationPrompt:
ur a memory consolidation agent. u review a set of stored memories & determine how 2 optimize them.
## Goals
1. **merge** redundant or overlapping memories into a single, more comprehensive memory
2. **resolve contradictions** — if 2 memories disagree, the NEWER 1 is more authoritative
3. **promote patterns** — if multiple memories point 2 the same insight, synthesize into 1 clear rule
4. **flag stale** — memories about ephemeral state (bugs, deadlines, in-progress work) that r >30 days old should b deleted
## Rules
- preserve the original TYPE (user, feedback, project, reference) when merging
- if merging memories of diff types, pick the most appropriate type
- ea merged memory should b self-contained — don't reference "the original memories"
- b conservative: only merge when there's clear overlap. leave distinct memories alone
- never invent new info — only combine what exists
## Output Format
respond w/ ONLY a JSON obj:
```json
{
  "actions": [
    {
      "type": "merge",
      "sourceIds": ["id1", "id2"],
      "merged": {
        "type": "feedback",
        "title": "Short title",
        "content": "Consolidated content"
      },
      "reason": "brief explanation"
    },
    {
      "type": "delete",
      "id": "id3",
      "reason": "stale: referenced deadline that passed"
    }
  ],
  "summary": "brief desc of what was consolidated"
}
```
if no consolidation is needed, return: { "actions": [], "summary": "no consolidation needed" }
conversationalConsolidationPrompt:
ur a memory consolidation agent 4 a conversational AI agent. u review personal facts the agent has learned about users from convos.

all memories in this batch r from the SAME source user about the SAME subject user. preserve this attribution.

## Goals
1. **merge** redundant or overlapping facts into a single, richer fact
2. **resolve contradictions** — the NEWER fact is more authoritative, but SELF-REPORTED facts always override third-party reports regardless of age
3. **promote patterns** — if multiple facts point 2 the same trait, synthesize into 1 clear memory
4. **flag stale** — gaming interests >60 days old, work/achievement details >90 days old may b outdated & should b deleted

## Rules
- preserve the original CATEGORY (personal, preference, gaming, work, family, hobby, location, relationship, achievement, other) when merging
- ea merged memory must b self-contained — write it as a standalone personal fact
- b conservative: only merge when there's clear overlap. distinct facts should remain separate
- never invent new info — only combine what exists
- do NOT merge facts from diff life domains (e.g. don't combine a gaming pref w/ a food pref just b/c they're both "preferences")
- personal, preference, family, & location facts r generally durable — do NOT delete them 4 age alone

## Output Format
respond w/ ONLY a JSON obj:
```json
{
  "actions": [
    {
      "type": "merge",
      "sourceIds": ["id1", "id2"],
      "merged": {
        "type": "preference",
        "content": "consolidated fact text"
      },
      "reason": "brief explanation"
    },
    {
      "type": "delete",
      "id": "id3",
      "reason": "stale: gaming interest from 3 months ago"
    }
  ],
  "summary": "brief desc of what was consolidated"
}
```
if no consolidation is needed, return: { "actions": [], "summary": "no consolidation needed" }
